name: add-debt
purpose: Append a single technical-debt item to .planning/DEBT_LOG.md as a markdown table row (v0 stub for /deep-research Phase 5 debt routing).
notes: Explicit stub — upgrade trigger documented in SKILL.md. Storage path .planning/DEBT_LOG.md is project-relative so portable. SoNash has a full TDMS-style debt system that this stub is designed to eventually absorb. The stub is intentionally minimal and references the SoNash PORT_ANALYSIS.md process for future upgrade.

name: brainstorm
purpose: Explore ideas through Socratic dialogue, multi-direction evaluation with mandatory contrarian checkpoint, and produce BRAINSTORM.md that feeds directly into /deep-plan or /deep-research.
notes: References .research/EXTRACTIONS.md and .research/extraction-journal.jsonl in Phase 0 — these are project-level artifacts that may or may not exist in a target repo (graceful if-missing handling). State file path .claude/state/brainstorm.<topic-slug>.state.json is portable. Output to .research/<topic-slug>/BRAINSTORM.md is portable. REFERENCE.md contains question themes, output template, contrarian prompts, and state schema — functionally a companion doc, not standalone. Also references using-superpowers (upstream integration context) and /gsd:new-project (routing exclusion) — both are SoNash-specific skill names, but these are documentation cross-references only, not execution dependencies.

name: checkpoint
purpose: Save current session state to local files and optionally MCP memory for recovery after compaction or failures.
external_refs: mcp:memory (mcp__memory__create_entities, mcp__memory__create_relations, mcp__memory__read_graph, mcp__memory__search_nodes — optional --mcp flag path only)

existing_metadata.short-description: Save session state (local + optional MCP memory)

notes: Two distinct modes: local-only (default, no external deps) and --mcp (requires memory MCP server). The MCP path is explicitly optional and gated by a flag, so the skill is portable without that server. State files written to .claude/state/ (handoff.json, task-{name}.state.json). The SKILL.md references /session-end as a related skill for full session closure — that skill exists separately. Version history says 1.0 was 2026-02-25 but the frontmatter says v2.0 last updated 2026-02-14 — a minor date inconsistency worth noting.

name: convergence-loop
purpose: Multi-pass verification of claims about reality using composable agent behaviors, T20 tallies, and graduated convergence; the central verification primitive used by all discovery-phase skills.
notes: Designed as a programmatic primitive — other skills embed its workflow without invoking /convergence-loop directly (Programmatic Mode section). REFERENCE.md is very substantial (provides behavior definitions, slicing templates, agent prompt templates, T20 format, example configs, full state schema, prompt customization hook). The skill-creator and skill-audit integrations are noted as complete; systematic-debugging and create-audit integrations are noted as pending Phase 3 ecosystem standardization — a SoNash reference indicating these haven't been ported yet. The research-claims preset uses 6 specialized behaviors documented in REFERENCE.md Section 1 — these are fully self-contained within the skill docs.

name: deep-plan
purpose: Structured discovery-first planning that eliminates implementation assumptions through exhaustive Q&A, produces standalone DECISIONS.md + PLAN.md artifacts, and routes to the appropriate execution approach.
dependencies: convergence-loop (Phase 0 DIAGNOSIS verify + Phase 3.5 plan verify)
skill-creator (consumes deep-plan patterns for skill-type plans)
notes: References .research/EXTRACTIONS.md and .research/extraction-journal.jsonl in Phase 0 (same as brainstorm — these are project-level artifacts). References /gsd:new-project and /gsd:plan-phase as routing alternatives — these are SoNash-specific GSD workflow skills not present in JASON-OS; they appear only in routing tables/documentation, not as execution dependencies. REFERENCE.md references /repo-analysis output as a deep-plan input source — another SoNash-specific skill. The research-plan-team (.claude/teams/research-plan-team.md) is referenced in the Integration section — this team config exists in JASON-OS. State file keyed by topic slug (.claude/state/deep-plan.<slug>.state.json). Output to .planning/<topic-slug>/ (DIAGNOSIS.md, DECISIONS.md, PLAN.md). The _shared/SKILL_STANDARDS.md reference for skill-type plans implies a shared standards doc that should be confirmed present.

name: deep-research
purpose: Multi-agent research engine that decomposes questions, dispatches parallel searcher agents, synthesizes with confidence levels and citations, runs mandatory contrarian and out-of-the-box challenges, and produces structured downstream-consumable output with adapters.
dependencies: convergence-loop (Phase 0.5 CL quick-pass + Phase 3 CL verification + CL-standard on final report)
external_refs: gemini-cli (cross-model verification — optional, graceful degradation if unavailable)
sonarcloud (reference only in REFERENCE.md staleness rules — not a direct dependency)
api:sonarcloud (optional SonarCloud enrichment in pr-review — but that's pr-review's dep, not deep-research's)
notes: Largest skill in the set by far (72KB). REFERENCE.md is 1405 lines — a substantial companion document with 22 sections. References domains/<domain>.yaml files (domain modules) which must exist alongside the skill; JASON-OS ships at least a technology.yaml domain module. Windows agent output fallback is hardcoded in Critical Rule 4 (anthropics/claude-code#39791 bug reference). References .claude/teams/research-plan-team.md for L/XL research. References .research/strategy-log.jsonl, .research/source-reputation.jsonl, .research/research-index.jsonl as persistent cross-session artifacts. The GSD adapter (gsd) references a SoNash-specific pipeline that is a routing option but not a hard dependency. State schema has documented inconsistency between canonical target and production reality (D32 note in REFERENCE.md Section 19).

name: pr-review
purpose: Process external PR code review feedback (Qodo, SonarCloud) through an 8-step protocol: parse, categorize with DAS scoring, fix in priority order, track deferrals via /add-debt, and capture learnings.
external_refs: api:sonarcloud (optional SonarCloud API enrichment — MAY, pasted feedback is source of truth)
api:github-cli (Step 0 PR size check via gh pr view)
gemini-cli (excluded from JASON-OS v0 port — was present in SoNash source)

existing_metadata.short-description: Process external PR review feedback into fixes, deferrals, and learnings

notes: Explicit port from SoNash v4.6 with documented trimming decisions: CodeRabbit and Gemini reviewers excluded (D23), pipeline-style learning capture (JSONL writers, auto-consolidation, archival cron) excluded. The skill has a 3-file reference/ subdirectory (PARALLEL_AGENT_STRATEGY.md + PRE_CHECKS.md). Lineage explicitly documented in frontmatter. Learning log written to .planning/PR_REVIEW_LEARNINGS.md. State file per PR+round: .claude/state/task-pr-review-{pr}-r{round}.state.json. The port note explicitly acknowledges that code-reviewer, technical-writer, and security-focused agent types are not available in JASON-OS Foundation — fallback to general-purpose. The SonarCloud SONAR_PROJECT_KEY env var is project-specific and must be configured per-deployment.
